skill.name: markdown-toc
skill.description: Generate and update tables of contents in Markdown files using documented Python command workflows, header level filters, and insertion modes.
skill.summary: Creates GitHub-compatible Markdown tables of contents for one file, many files, or recursive documentation folders.
skill.author: Emasoft

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many Ruby backtick execution and weak cryptography patterns, but review found these are Markdown code fences, inline code, option tables, and the string "markdown", not executable Ruby or cryptographic code. The skill does instruct users to run Python commands that modify Markdown files, so the remaining risk is low and limited to intended local file changes.

low_findings.title: Intended Local Command Execution
low_findings.description: The skill documents Python command invocations for generating or updating Markdown tables of contents. This is expected behavior for the skill, but it can modify local Markdown files if users run commands without dry-run review.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file clearly provides Python commands that operate on local Markdown paths. The commands are documented examples for the skill purpose, with no evidence of hidden payloads, network use, or secret access.

low_findings.title: Static Backtick and Weak-Crypto Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby backtick execution and weak cryptography findings come from Markdown fences, inline code examples, and Markdown-related text. No Ruby execution, cryptographic operation, or malicious command construction was found in SKILL.md.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The suspicious tokens are inside Markdown documentation and examples. The reviewed context contains no Ruby code and no cryptographic API usage.

dangerous_patterns.title: File-Modifying Python Commands
dangerous_patterns.description: Examples show Python commands that can update README and documentation Markdown files. Users should run dry-run mode first and review changes before applying recursive updates.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands are explicit and relevant to the skill function. Risk is limited to local file edits because no network, environment variable, or credential handling behavior is evidenced.

content.value_statement: Large Markdown files become difficult to scan when headings change. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code generate or refresh GitHub-compatible tables of contents with controlled insertion modes.
actual_capabilities: Generates Markdown tables of contents from document headings.
Supports dry-run previews before changing files.
Controls included heading levels with minimum and maximum options.
Supports top, automatic, and marker-based insertion modes.
Handles single files, multiple paths, glob patterns, and recursive documentation folders.
Creates GitHub-style anchor links from Markdown headings.
limitations: The package contains instructions only; the referenced Python script is not present in the scanned file tree.
Recursive updates can change many files if dry-run mode is skipped.
Anchor behavior may differ for duplicate headings on some Markdown renderers.
The skill is focused on Markdown tables of contents, not full documentation restructuring.

use_cases.title: Refresh a Project README
use_cases.description: Update a README table of contents after adding or renaming sections.

use_cases.title: Standardize Documentation Folders
use_cases.description: Apply consistent TOC markers and heading depth across many Markdown guides.

use_cases.title: Preview Navigation Changes
use_cases.description: Run a dry preview to confirm the generated TOC before writing changes.

prompt_templates.title: Add a Basic TOC
prompt_templates.prompt: Add a table of contents to README.md using the default heading levels. Show me the command and use dry-run first.

prompt_templates.title: Update Documentation Recursively
prompt_templates.prompt: Refresh tables of contents for all Markdown files under docs. Preview the changes first, then explain what will be modified.

prompt_templates.title: Use Marker-Based Insertion
prompt_templates.prompt: Update the table of contents only between TOC markers in the docs folder. Keep the existing document structure unchanged.

prompt_templates.title: Tune Heading Depth
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a shallow table of contents for guide files using only H2 headings, and generate a deeper one for README.md through H4.

output_examples.input: A README has sections for Installation, Usage, Advanced Usage, and Contributing.
output_examples.output: The skill produces a table of contents with links to each section and nested entries for deeper headings.

output_examples.input: A docs folder contains many Markdown guides with inconsistent TOC placement.
output: A dry-run preview lists the affected files.
Marker mode updates only the intended TOC areas.
The result uses consistent heading depth across guides.

output_examples.input: A document has YAML frontmatter before the first heading.
output_examples.output: The table of contents is placed after the frontmatter or another selected insertion point.

best_practices: Run dry-run mode before writing changes, especially for recursive updates.
Use marker mode when a document needs precise TOC placement.
Choose a small heading range for long documents to keep navigation readable.
anti_patterns: Do not run recursive updates on a repository before reviewing the dry-run output.
Do not include every heading level when the TOC becomes longer than the content it helps navigate.
Do not rely on generated anchors without checking duplicate headings in important documents.

faq.question: What does this skill generate?
faq.answer: It generates Markdown tables of contents with links based on document headings.

faq.question: Can it update multiple files?
faq.answer: Yes. The documented workflow supports multiple paths, glob patterns, and recursive Markdown folders.

faq.question: Can I preview changes first?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill documents dry-run mode so users can review output before files are modified.

faq.question: Where can the TOC be inserted?
faq.answer: It can be inserted automatically, at the top, or between explicit marker comments.

faq.question: Does it need external services?
faq.answer: No evidence of network services was found in the reviewed skill file.

faq.question: Is the referenced script included?
faq.answer: No. The scanned file tree contains SKILL.md only, so the referenced script was not available for review.
